issuetype.description: A big user story that needs to be broken down. Created by Jira Software - do not edit or delete.
issuetype.name: Feature

content.text: Description:
Embedding SDK offers an interaction with panel stacks by embedding them as a stand alone panel.
The customer requires having this functionality available when using the Native embedding SDK
Expected Behavior:
support interaction with Pannel Stack via Native Embedding API
Steps to Reproduce:
Example workflows is not present in the playground for native SDK nor there is any documentation related to interacting with panels in the embdedding SDK documentation.
related links
https://microstrategy.github.io/playground/
https://microstrategy.github.io/embedding-sdk-docs/add-functionality/panel-stacks/
Business Impact:

[1] How many users do you intend on rolling this feature/functionality out to? What are their business roles? – btw 3500 - 5000 users. They are Account executives, CVS clients, CVS Admins and Leadership

[2] Do you have any viable workarounds for this behavior? – The workaround is to create custom UI component to mimic the panel stack on the front end; however, business team sees this as a short term solution as it’s create maintenance/performance overhead on the front end.

[3] Does this request represent a critical functionality? If so, could you offer more details? – This request is important as this is part of the major initiative to improve performance, remove overhead and reduce latency during high concurrency on the front end for the next PROD release.

[4] What is the business requirement for this enhancement request, as well as the use-case? – The requirement is to allow end user to switch different views of the same data. Currently the custom front end UI is created to mimic the panel stack on the front end; however, business team sees this as a short term solution as it’s create maintenance/performance overhead on the front end.
[5] Are there any pending go-lives or deliverables which hinge on this functionality? – The next major PORD release be around end of 2025.
Troubleshooting Steps:
As of May 12, Dianne to provide more details of the working scenario and clarify the enhancement details.
As of May 29
Support submitted an enhancement request

fields.summary: [Customer Committed] Ability to embed panel stack as a single embedding component in Native Embedding - feature currently available in regular embedding SDK

content.text: Account: CVS Pharmacy, Inc., Owner: Alan Decunto (CSS), Creator: null
Subject:
Ability to embed panel stack as a single embedding component in Native Embedding - feature currently available in regular embedding SDK
Business Impact:

[1] How many users do you intend on rolling this feature/functionality out to? What are their business roles? – btw 3500 - 5000 users. They are Account executives, CVS clients, CVS Admins and Leadership

[2] Do you have any viable workarounds for this behavior? – The workaround is to create custom UI component to mimic the panel stack on the front end; however, business team sees this as a short term solution as it’s create maintenance/performance overhead on the front end.

[3] Does this request represent a critical functionality? If so, could you offer more details? – This request is important as this is part of the major initiative to improve performance, remove overhead and reduce latency during high concurrency on the front end for the next PROD release.

[4] What is the business requirement for this enhancement request, as well as the use-case? – The requirement is to allow end user to switch different views of the same data. Currently the custom front end UI is created to mimic the panel stack on the front end; however, business team sees this as a short term solution as it’s create maintenance/performance overhead on the front end.
[5] Are there any pending go-lives or deliverables which hinge on this functionality? – The next major PORD release be around end of 2025.
Description:
Embedding SDK offers an interaction with panel stacks by embedding them as a stand alone panel.
The customer requires having this functionality available when using the Native embedding SDK
@Stan Gordiienko is this an urgent customer requirement?

@sgordiienko @ @qdong The Production Release of this customer enhancement request [US623244] was updated to 25.12 be the same as that of its parent Feature [F43484] so it matches when it will be delivered along with the Feature.
Successfully migrated from Rally
: Ability to embed panel stack as a single embedding component in Native Embedding - feature currently available in regular embedding SDK
Migration Exceptions:
2025-08-18 02:51:14: Iteration not found for Rally item US623244. Skipping setting iteration.
Hello

As we’ve discussed, please note that this case has been added to the Escalation-Active list today. The customer has requested the ability to embed panel stacks as a single embedding component in the Native Embedding SDK. Will you please review it with
Thanks,
CC

This ER was escalated today, and I will convert it to a feature. Can you help me understand how urgent this ER is to us, because we have already made the Q4 dev plan, if this ER must be done in Q4, we have to make some plan changes.
cc
Thank you for giving us heads up. We have a meeting with CSM tomorrow to understand why is this a requirement for 25.10 (the consultant and CSM mentioned they are asking this in 25.10)
Also it is not clear how does this fit into the existing list of priorities for CVS, especially CS0940895/
which is also what
's team is working on.
In the meantime,
, as I will find out more about the priorities at CVS, we may want to review your team’s Q4 plan for potential tradeoffs.
cc:
We are reviewing the requirements and scope with PM
today and spiking the feasibility of the Q4 release.
cc
Per discussion with

, as value parameters are already supported in Native Embedding, this related case can be deferred. Panel support remains important, but considering current priorities, we’ll plan to move this case to Q2.
cc
Disassociated from ServiceNow Case:

Team, we’ve been in several talks with the account team and the customer themselves (CVS), and this ER has come up as their higher priority, since not having it in Native Embedded SDK means they have to incur significant technical debt in their implementation. We’re wondering what it would take to deliver this one sooner, if the team can. They have recently successfully adopted CMC, so even a monthly release delivery could greatly benefit them. Could you please advice on when it’s the earliest we could deliver this, and what potential priority conflicts or trade-offs we could discuss to make it happen, from your perspective (i.e. based on level of effort)? Thanks! cc
Hi Stan, the panel stack can be nested recursively. So does the customer require us to support embedding a panel stack that is inside another panel stack?  cc

Good question - so far their use case does not involve nesting Panel Stacks. They are using Panel Stack to build multiple versions of a visualization, e.g. each panel has a different visualization (different groups of attributes/metrics and viz-level filters). The workflow they need to implement is that when business users interact with CVS’s embedding application, they select some options to determine the type of analysis they need, and based on that the application has logic to invoke specific panel, or panels from the Panel Stack, as to return those visualizations embedding them (in the same page, that is). Hope this makes sense. Pls let us know if you and team need any more details. Thank you for the investigation.
P.S. Would it be possible to get an ETA on when the customer can expect an update from us in terms of the feasible timeline for this ER?
cc
Thanks for your detailed explanations Alejandro. So I think we can only support the non-nested panel stacks in this feature.

However, I am not sure about “the application has logic to invoke specific panel, or panels from the Panel Stack, as to return those visualizations embedding them (in the same page, that is)”. Does it mean the customer needs the Native Embedding SDK to provide an API to select a panel from the client application side?
And if it’s true, does the customer require some event when he manually switches a panel in the embedded panel stack? Though the description provides the iframe embedding docs about panel stack
as a reference, the Native Embedding SDK is different, so I think we’d better have a complete list about what else need to do besides embedding the panel stack to a container.
cc
We already have informed the customer that the delivery of this item in Q1 is unlikely. But the customer is on containerized deployment, hence the question is can we consider 26.04 potentially?
Waiting for their most urgent item for 6 months is a bit of a stretch, so at least if we could explain what the complexity is, it could help us explain the wait.
Cc:
This feature is still quite complex and requires substantial effort. However, thanks to

’s research over the past period, we now have a relatively clear solution. Therefore, moving the release up to 04 should be feasible. That said, the schedule will remain very tight, and 04 is already the earliest release date we can realistically commit to.
Move this feature to 26.04
cc
Thank you very much for re-prioritizing this effort sooner.
I am going to inform the account team that we have tentatively scheduled this work for 26.04, but due to complexity this is an item at risk.
cc:

Hi guys, we are still in the design phase of this feature. Here are some detailed requirements analysis. Could you please help confirm whether it’s complete and exactly fits the customer’s requirements? Thanks!
What we will do:
The Native Embedding SDK API should support embedding the panel stacks.
We will enhance the current
MstrDossier.refresh(props)
API to do this. Now the user can input the panel stack key and its paired container inside
props
, like
The original Native Embedding SDK API
MstrDossier.applyFilter(filter)
and
MstrDossier.applyFilters(filters)
should also work for the embedded panel stacks.
These 2 APIs should also refresh the related panel stacks after the filter is applied if the panel stack is in the affected scope of the filter.
The Native Embedding SDK should provide a new API to switch panels for embedded panel stacks. This API can be used to switch the visualization(panel) that the user shows.
We will provide a new API
MstrDossier.switchPanel(panelKey)
to do this.
The Native Embedding SDK can provide an event to notify the SDK user that a panel is switched if the user manually switches the panel on the panel stack.
This is in the iframe SDK functionality, but needs extra confirmation. I am not sure whether the customer wants to keep the panel switch button on UI, and whether they need this event.
What we will not do:
We will not support these functionalities as they don’t benefit the customer’s use case and will add much more complexity:
Embed the visualization inside a panel stack individually.
This isn’t supported in the past, and we will not enable it in this feature.
Embed the nested panel stack individually.
We will not support embedding a nested panel stack whose parent contains a panel stack directly.
In other words, we will only support embedding the visualizations and the panel stacks that are directly under a dossier page.
cc
, based on the initial review, this is perfectly acceptable.
We are meeting with CVS account team tomorrow morning (US time) and will confirm with them as well.

I’m forgetting the details, but I recall that Rey Rey raised a potential concern in how multiple manipulations can be sent in the Embedding API to be able to embed individual panels more effectively - was this related to how the customer expects to be able to set filters that target the PS, say? Rey Rey, seems like I can tag you here in JIRA, so perhaps you can shed more light on what the requirement you brought up was, so our ENG team also considers it? Thanks!
you are 100% correct.
Last year some time around 25.04-25.06 timeline we added support to submit multiple attribute element filter selections all in one call, therefore reducing network latency.
Ray Rey can already foresee that adding a new API
MstrDossier.switchPanel(panelKey)
will be a move in the wrong direction, as adding an additional call would again increase network latency.
Instead, can we be proactive with CVS, and consider the capability of panel stack native embedding via the existing
MstrDossier.applyFilters
This would allow the customer to perform multiple selections (for example an attribute element selector and the panel selection) in one operation.
Besides this concern, the rest of the requirements would be sufficient for CVS, at least for the initial release.
cc:
Thanks
for tagging me, looks like I am able to contribute in Jira
thanks for providing the great details!
One more question for
is whether it’s feasible to interact with Panel Stack using Panel Stack selector via the existing MstrDossier.applyFilters functionality.
Hi Stan, does the CVS workflow indeed contain a scenario that needs to switch the panel and apply the filter at the same time?
If they indeed have such workflow, I think we can consider releasing a new API like
applyManipulations()
to apply filters and panel switch at the same time, instead provide
switchPanel()
only.  cc
Yes our new API can switch the panel. For the Panel Stack selector, it uses the same REST API as manually switching on the panel stack, so we don’t need to consider it especially.
We can use the new API like
applyManipulations()
to support the panel switch. For the old
applyFilters()
, I don’t think we should change it as it’s not designed for panel switch and there are data structure incompatibilities.

Thank you for your patience. CVS has confirmed that out of their current dashboard implementations, couple of those require additional page selectors such as (Metric qualification selector, Attribute element selctor), besides a panel selector. That is not including any future potential implementations that they may need.

So, the ideal preference would be to come up with a way to make such selections on metrics, attributes and panel selector all in one call, as CVS is very sensitive about the performance, which gets impacted with increased number of calls.
cc:
Got it, thanks! Then I will implement a new API that would combine all these manipulations together.  cc
And if we do like this, do you think the switch panel event is still needed? I think we’d better remove it if the customer doesn’t have related workflows.
Hi Rey Rey, can you help us confirm
's last comment above, is this indeed a fair assumption? Thanks!
and

If my understanding is correct, the switch panel event is intended to support direct end-user interaction with the panel stack after it has been rendered in the view. For the CVS use case, direct user interaction with the panel switch is not required, as the panel transition will be initiated programmatically rather than by the end user. Please let me know if this understanding is incorrect.
Thanks for your confirmation! Then I will remove this part from the design if there are no further requirement changes.
